subject: Joint Distributions
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ have joint density $f_{XY}(x, y) = \begin{cases} \frac{4}{9} & x \le y \le 3 - x, 0 \le x \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ (a) Compute the marginal densities of $X$ and $Y$. (b) Compute $\Pr(Y > 2X)$. (c) Are $X$ and $Y$ independent?
answer: (a) We have $f_X(x) = \int_x^{3-x} \frac{4}{9}\,dy = \frac{4}{3} - \frac{8}{9}x, 0 \le x \le \frac{3}{2}$, 0 otherwise and $f_Y(y) = \begin{cases} \frac{4}{9}y & 0 \le y \le \frac{3}{2} \\ \frac{4}{9}(3-y) & \frac{3}{2} \le y \le 3 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ (b) $\frac{2}{3}$ (c) $X$ and $Y$ are dependent
explanation: Answer: (a) We have $f_X(x) = \int_x^{3-x} \frac{4}{9}\,dy = \frac{4}{3} - \frac{8}{9}x, 0 \le x \le \frac{3}{2}$, 0 otherwise and $f_Y(y) = \begin{cases} \frac{4}{9}y & 0 \le y \le \frac{3}{2} \\ \frac{4}{9}(3-y) & \frac{3}{2} \le y \le 3 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ (b) $\frac{2}{3}$ (c) $X$ and $Y$ are dependent
